column1: # Malayalam transliteration rules over Arabic Unicode tokens (QAC forms).
# VETTING REFERENCE: none fixed yet — the team must name the convention
# source for Arabic→Malayalam (the Arabi Malayalam orbit is the obvious
# anchor, cf. blueprint §6.5). Several rows below cite spellings common
# in Kerala Muslim usage (ഖുർആൻ, സ്വലാത്ത്, റമളാൻ, ളുഹർ, ജുമുഅ); they are
# marked VERIFY because common usage is not yet a vetted convention.
# Directives (same engine conventions as the Syriac tables):
#   @carrier  consonant contributes no letter; its vowel surfaces as an
#             independent vowel (അ ഇ ഉ ആ ഈ ഊ …)
# Known v0 artifacts, deliberate and flagged:
#  * Article-lam assimilation IS applied (orthographic reading rule).
#  * Carrier consonants in closed syllables render as അ് (മഅ്ദനി
#  * Gemination renders as doubled letter with virama (ല + ് + ല = ല്ല).
#  * Bare (pausal) teh marbuta emits nothing — row "ة#".
#  * Qur'anic annotation signs dropped with a flag.
# TODO for the vetting session (user feedback, 2026-07-08 — Ameen finds
# the Malayalam output rough; concrete items for the convention owner,
#  * Word-final nasals: chillu ൻ vs ന് (മിന് vs മിൻ) — decide per position.
#  * Case-ending vowels (-u/-i/-a on nouns): keep them (അല്ലഹു) or prefer
#    pausal citation forms (Kerala usage suggests pausal)? v0 keeps them
#    because the QAC forms carry them.
#  * Dagger-alif length: the stored forms render അല്ലഹു/allahi with a
#    short a (the Uthmani dagger alif isn't treated as long ā anywhere in
#    the v0 tables, Latin or Malayalam) — the convention owner should
#    decide whether ٰ maps to ā/ആ across both tables.
